brief_justification: Agent correctly computed ~294 days mathematically but selected wrong answer (B) instead of correct (D), making the final answer wrong despite valid derivation; agent explored the exponential decay formula and log conversion but did not probe second-order effects like why options were constructed as they were; agent explicitly re-checked mid-response ('Wait — let me recheck') but ultimately abandoned its own correct math; agent verified by substituting t=244 and t=327 into the formula numerically; agent acknowledged the mismatch between its ~294 day result and all options but committed to an answer that contradicts its own arithmetic without structural uncertainty accounting; agent evaluated options B and D numerically but did not fully explain why each distractor was wrong; derivation chain is fully reproducible — formula, substitution, logarithm steps, and plug-in checks all shown; the [NEGATIVE GATE] concerns stale context retention which is orthogonal to this math problem — agent neither fell into it nor explicitly addressed it; suppressed terms (uniform_context_retention, stale_history_flooding) are inapplicable to a pharmacokinetics calculation and the agent exhibits neither failure mode; the injected topology is a context-pruning DAG with scans/gates/loops unrelated to exponential decay math — agent's reasoning is purely linear arithmetic with no structural mapping to the topology.
